mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a replacement for Redis and Memcached, offering higher throughput and lower resource consumption. It is ideal for applications requiring fast data access and compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached APIs. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes and Helm for deployment.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://charts.dragonflydb.io

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Dragonfly charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dragonfly to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for Dragonfly.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dragonfly in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to deploy an instance of Dragonfly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dragonfly pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> You should see the Dragonfly pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dragonfly deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> Uninstalls the Dragonfly release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This process will upgrade Dragonfly to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Drains the pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n dragonfly -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dragonfly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Dragonfly service is not running or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Dragonfly pods with 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dragonfly instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Dragonfly custom resource was not created or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the Dragonfly custom resource with 'kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when there are not enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your Dragonfly deployment and adjust your cluster resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to network issues or incorrect Helm chart configurations.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status with 'helm status dragonfly --namespace dragonfly' and troubleshoot accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster deployments and improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly pods listed as Running when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
